news.summary: The meat packaging industry has been greatly impacted by the rapid growth of e-commerce platforms and organized retail chains. The increasing shift of consumers towards online shopping for groceries has led to a surge in demand for top-notch, tamper-proof, and visually attractive meat packaging.
news.headline: Global plastic-based meat packaging market to hit $14.4 billion by 2034 at 3.9% CAGR, driven by e-commerce growth.
US Consulate General in Pakistan's Lahore directs staff to shelter in place

news.summary: Titan SA, a listed company on Euronext Brussels, Euronext Paris, and ATHEX as "TITC," has released its first quarter 2025 financial results. The company reported a positive beginning to the year, with sales increasing by 2.4% to €638.4 million. This growth was attributed to consistent pricing strategies, stable cement volumes, and expanding volumes in core products, despite challenges from adverse weather conditions affecting markets in the US and Southeast Europe. Titan SA also experienced robust EBITDA growth, surging by 11.7% to €122.6 million during the same period.
news.headline: TITAN Group's first quarter financial results show sales up 2.4% to €638.4m and EBITDA growth of 11.7% to €122.6m.

news.summary: NICE (Nasdaq: NICE) is set to release its first quarter financial results on May 15, 2025, before the NASDAQ Stock Exchange opens. A conference call will follow on the same day, with timings as follows: 8:30 AM Eastern Time, 1:30 PM UK Time, and 3:30 PM Israel Time. The call will be broadcast live on NICE's website at https://www.nice.com/company/investors/upcoming-event. Participants can register for the webcast or dial-in using the provided link.
news.headline: NICE to announce Q1 2025 results on May 15, 2025, before NASDAQ opening. Conference call at 8:30 AM - Eastern, 1:30 PM - UK, and 3:30 PM - Israel. Webcast available on company website.

This global competition welcomes creatives, filmmakers, and videomakers eager to utilize AI tools to craft short films. The festival aims to delve into the intersection of AI and film production, supporting emerging talent. Entries from around the world are invited to participate in this innovative opportunity for showcasing creativity.

The podcast streams live five days a week on YouTube and X.

news.summary: Cryptocurrency markets rallied due to a weaker dollar and the rise in gold prices. Bitcoin (BTC) hit $87k amid news of potential $2 billion BTC buy announcement. Solana (SOL) overtook Ethereum (ETH) in market cap staked, with SOL/ETH weekly close hitting an all-time high. Notable investments include Metaplanet's $28m BTC purchase and Abraxas Capital's $250m BTC acquisition. Public firms now hold $56bn BTC, a 16% increase year-on-year in Q1. Euro Central Bank (ECB) plans to launch Digital Euro in Oct 2025, while Standard Chartered predicts $2T in stablecoins by 2028. Charles Schwab plans to offer crypto trading within a year. Only 68% of ByBit hacked funds remain traceable. Key discussions on the FOMO HOUR podcast include the impact of gold and dollar on Bitcoin trends, market sentiment predictions for 2025, and controversies surrounding meme coins and content coins in the industry. Vitalik Buterin proposes RISC-V to replace ETH’s EVM, with opinions on SOL having a better roadmap than ETH. There are insights on Powell's economic decisions, along with analyses of Bitcoin accumulation by major players and the future IPO of Circle. The market also saw launches like Hashkey's XRP tracker fund and Canary's filed staked TRX ETF. Live streaming and discussions happen on YouTube and X, covering daily news, market updates, and events in the crypto industry.
news.headline: Crypto surges on weaker dollar, gold rises, stocks falter. BTC hits $87k, SOL overtakes ETH, $2bn BTC buy likely. ECB to introduce Digital Euro by Oct 2025. Public firms hold $56bn BTC. Schwab to offer crypto trading. Metaplanet, Abraxas Capital make significant BTC purchases.станMart forecasts $2T stablecoins by 2028. ByBit hack funds 68% traceable. Enjoy FOMO HOUR for latest updates. #cryptonews

news.summary: A recent report indicates that the global economy is expected to contract by 4.4% in 2020 due to the coronavirus pandemic. This represents the worst decline since the Great Depression in the 1930s. It is estimated that the world GDP will decrease by $12 trillion by the end of the year. The lockdown measures have led to a sharp increase in unemployment, with approximately 300 million jobs lost worldwide. Developing countries are particularly affected, facing challenges such as food insecurity and lack of healthcare access. The report highlights the importance of international cooperation and policy support to mitigate the economic impact and promote recovery.
news.headline: Himax Technologies, Inc. announces cash dividend for fiscal year 2024.

news.summary: Founder and CEO Mohammad Raafi Hossain of Fasset discusses with Decrypt about the company's goal to promote financial inclusion and facilitate tokenized assets in developing economies. He also highlights the introduction of Own, the inaugural Ethereum Layer 2 protocol designed to enable the generation, administration, and dissemination of tokenized Real-World Assets (RWAs).
news.headline: CEO of Fasset believes in universal wealth-building rights. Talks to Decrypt about tokenized assets in emerging markets, launching Own on Ethereum Layer 2.

news.summary: Bitcoin (BTC) has surged back to $93.6k with a 5% increase, while Ethereum (ETH) rose by 11% to $1810 and Solana (SOL) increased by 9% to $152. The top gainers include IMX, SUI, BONK, ENA, and CRO. Bitcoin ETFs saw an inflow of $913 million, while ETH ETFs saw an inflow of $39 million. The crypto market rallied on Trump's comments regarding Jerome Powell, leading to over $500 million in liquidations. Bitcoin flipped Alphabet to become the 5th largest asset and is being used to hedge systemic risk, according to StanChart. SUI and ETH led the rally in Layer 1 coins, while XRP ledger suffered a hacker attack. Trump Media and CryptoCom are set to partner on ETFs, and Cantor, Softbank, and Tether have formed a $3 billion Bitcoin vehicle. DeFi Development Corp purchased $11.5 million worth of SOL. US support for crypto poses risks for Europe as ECB warns, $1 billion more USDT has been minted at Tether, and ING is the latest traditional finance firm developing a stablecoin. The Malaysian PM met with CZ to discuss crypto developments. Live streams and content are available on YouTube, Spotify, and X. #bitcoin #crypto #podcast.

BTC/ETH ETFs up: +$913mn, +$39mn. $500m liquidated in rally, Bitcoin now 5th largest asset. Join FOMO HOUR for daily updates on YouTube and X.

news.summary: The Federal Reserve has ended its crackdown on cryptocurrencies, with Bitcoin remaining stable and the SUI coin leading gains amongst Level 1 cryptocurrencies. The Fed has also lifted the reporting mandate for banks in the crypto sector. Bitcoin's supply on exchanges is at its lowest since November 2018. Cantor stock surged after Capital 21's announcement while CME Group plans to launch XRP futures on May 19. The US government has postponed decisions on DOT and HBAR ETFs, with Citi projecting a $3.7 trillion stablecoin market by 2030. New Hampshire Senate cleared a Bitcoin bill, Tether increased its stake in Juventus FC to 10%, and MetaMask now allows users to choose gas tokens. AT&T customers can connect to the Helium Network, and Coinbase is collaborating with PayPal to promote PYUSD. Securitize and Mantle are collaborating on a crypto fund, while Binance has launched a points system for airdrops. The FOMO HOUR podcast covers the latest news and updates in the crypto and macro space, streaming live on YouTube and X from Monday to Friday. Links to join and follow the hosts are provided in the article.
